column7: Expected Result
column15: Steps

column7:
• The Username input field should be visible.
  • The Email input field should be visible.
  • The Password input field should be visible.
  • The Register button should be visible and accessible.
column15: Click on the Registration botton

column7:
• User should be authenticated successfully
  • Application should redirect user to homepage/dashboard
  • No access or authorization error should occur
column15:
• Click Continue with Google button
  • Select a Google account
  • Complete Google authentication process

column2: Successful User Registration with Valid Data
column7:
• The user should be successfully registered.
  • The system should create a new user account.
column15:
• Enter a valid username in the Username field.
  • Enter a valid and unique email address in the Email field.
  • Enter a valid password in the Password field.
  • Click on the Register button.

column7:
• The registration should fail.
  • The user should remain on the Registration page.
column15:
• Enter a valid username in the Username field.
  • Enter an already registered email address in the Email field.
  • Enter a valid password in the Password field.
  • Click on the Register button.

column7:
• The registration should fail.
  • The system should not allow account creation with an empty username.
  • A validation error message should be displayed under the Username field (e.g., “Username is required”).
  • The user should remain on the Registration page.
column15:
1. Leave the Username field empty.
  2. Enter a valid email address in the Email field.
  3. Enter a valid password in the Password field.
  4. Click on the Register button.

column7:
• The system should validate the minimum length requirement for the Username field.
  • If 1 character is not allowed, registration should fail.
  • A validation error message should be displayed (e.g., “Username must be at least 2 characters”).
  • The user should remain on the Registration page.
column15:
• Enter a 1 character username in the Username field.
  • Enter a valid email address in the Email field.
  • Enter a valid password in the Password field.
  • Click on the Register button.

column7:
• The system should validate the maximum length constraint for the Username field.
  • Registration should fail if the username exceeds the allowed limit.
  • A validation error message should be displayed (e.g., “Username must not exceed 32 characters”).
  • The user should remain on the Registration page.
column15:
1. Enter a username that exceeds the maximum allowed character limit (32 character)
  2. Enter a valid email address in the Email field.
  3. Enter a valid password in the Password field.
  4. Click on the Register button.

column7:
• The system should validate the Username field for allowed character rules.
  • Registration should fail if invalid characters are used.
  • A clear validation error message should be displayed (e.g., “Username contains invalid characters”).
  • The user should remain on the Registration page.
column15:
1. Enter a username containing invalid characters
  2. Enter a valid email address in the Email field.
  3. Enter a valid password in the Password field.
  4. Click on the Register button.

column7:
• The system should prevent registration with an empty Email field.
  • A validation error message should be displayed under the Email field (e.g., “Email is required”).
  • The user should remain on the Registration page.
column15:
• Enter a valid username in the Username field.
  • Leave the Email field empty.
  • Enter a valid password in the Password field.
  • Click on the Register button.

column7:
• The system should validate the Email field format.
  • Registration should fail due to invalid email format.
  • A clear validation error message should be displayed (e.g., “Please enter a valid email address”).
  • The user should remain on the Registration page.
column15:
1. Enter a valid username in the Username field.
  2. Enter an invalid email format in the Email field (e.g., dmail.com, user@, user@com, @mail.com).
  3. Enter a valid password in the Password field.
  4. Click on the Register button.

column7:
• The system should prevent registration when the Password field is empty.
  • A validation error message should be displayed under the Password field (e.g., “Password is required”).
  • The user should remain on the Registration page.
column15:
• Enter a valid username in the Username field.
  • Enter a valid email address in the Email field.
  • Leave the Password field empty.
  • Click on the Register button.

column7:
• The system should validate the minimum password length requirement.
  • Registration should fail if the password is shorter than 8 characters.
  • A validation error message should be displayed (e.g., “Password must be at least 8 characters long”).
  • The user should remain on the Registration page.
column15:
• Enter a valid username in the Username field.
  • Enter a valid email address in the Email field.
  • Enter a password with fewer than 8 characters
  • Click on the Register button.

column7:
• The system should validate the maximum password length constraint.
  • Registration should fail if the password exceeds 64 characters.
  • A validation error message should be displayed (e.g., “Password must not exceed 64 characters”).
  • The user should remain on the Registration page.
column15:
• Enter a valid username in the Username field.
  • Enter a valid email address in the Email field.
  • Enter a password longer than 64 characters.
  • Click on the Register button.

column7:
• The system should validate the password according to security rules.
  • Registration should fail due to weak or invalid password.
  • A clear validation error message should be displayed 
  • The user should remain on the Registration page.
column15:
1. Enter a valid username in the Username field.
  2. Enter a valid email address in the Email field.
  3. Enter an invalid password (e.g. contains spaces)
  4. Click on the Register button.

column7:
• The login form should be displayed.
  • The Email input field should be visible.
  • The Password input field should be visible.
  • The Log in button should be visible.
column15: Click on the Log in botton

column2: Successfull User Log in with Valid Data
column7: • The user should be successfully logged in.
column15:
• Enter a valid email address in the Email field.
  • Enter the correct password in the Password field.
  • Click on the Log in button.

column7:
• The login attempt should fail.
  • The system should not authenticate the user.
  • An appropriate error message should be displayed (e.g., “User not found” or “Invalid credentials”).
  • The user should remain on the Login page.
column15:
1. Enter an unregistered email address in the Email field.
  2. Enter a valid password in the Password field 
  3. Click on the Log in button.

column2: Login with Incorrect Password for Registered Email
column7:
• The login attempt should fail.
  • The system should not authenticate the user.
  • A generic error message should be displayed (e.g., “Invalid credentials”).
column15:
• Enter a registered email address in the Email field.
  • Enter an incorrect password in the Password field.
  • Click on the Log in button.

column7:
• The login attempt should be prevented.
  • The system should not allow submission with empty fields.
  • Validation error messages should be displayed under both Email and Password fields (e.g., “Email is required”, “Password is required”).
  • The user should remain on the Login page.
  • No authentication request should be successfully processed.
column15:
• Leave the Email field empty.
  • Leave the Password field empty.
  • Click on the Log in button.

column7:
• User session should be terminated
  • User should be redirected to login page
column15: Click Log Out button

column7:
1. The page should load successfully without any errors and the homepage should be displayed.
  2. The application name, logo, and tagline should be visible and aligned according to the design.
  3. The Registration button should be clickable and redirect the user to the registration form.
  4. The Log in button should be clickable and redirect the user to the login form.
column15:
1. Open the application homepage URL in a browser.
  2. Verify the presence of the application name, logo, and a motivational tagline.
  3. Verify the presence of the Registration button and click on it.
  4. Verify the presence of the Log in button and click on it.

column16:
Step Description:
  • Set viewport width to ≤ 320px and verify layout
  • Gradually increase to 320px – 374px and observe flexible behavior
  • Set viewport to 375px – 767px (mobile design range)
  • Set viewport to 768px – 1279px (tablet design range)
  • Set viewport to ≥ 1280px (desktop design range)
  • Observe layout consistency while resizing between breakpoints
Expected Result:
  • At all breakpoints, layout should adapt according to design rules
  • No horizontal scrolling should appear at any resolution
  • UI elements should remain within viewport boundaries
  • Text should wrap correctly and remain readable
  • Components should not overlap or break layout
  • Header/sidebar behavior should remain stable across sizes
  • Transitions between breakpoints should be smooth and consistent
column18:
1.
  • At all breakpoints, layout should adapt according to design rules
  • No horizontal scrolling should appear at any resolution
  • UI elements should remain within viewport boundaries
  • Text should wrap correctly and remain readable
  • Components should not overlap or break layout
  • Header/sidebar behavior should remain stable across sizes
  • Transitions between breakpoints should be smooth and consistent

  • Set viewport to 375px – 767px (mobile design range)

  • Set viewport to ≥ 1280px (desktop design range)

column2: Eye Icon Functionality on Registration Page
column7:

  • When the eye icon is clicked, the password should become visible in text.
  • When clicked again, the password should return to masked state.
column15:

  • Click on the eye icon next to the Password field.
  • Click on the eye icon again.

column2: Eye Icon Functionality on Login Page
column7:
• By default, the password should be masked (••••••).
  • When the eye icon is clicked, the password should become visible in text.
  • When clicked again, the password should be masked again.
column15:
1. Enter a valid password in the Password field.
  2. Click on the eye icon next to the Password field.
  3. Click on the eye icon again.

column7:

  • No UI inconsistencies or layout issues should occur in any theme.

column7:

    • A User Name field pre-filled with registered user data.
    • An Email field pre-filled with registered user data.
    • A Password field pre-filled or masked (based on design/security rules).
    • A Send button at the bottom of the modal.

column7:

column7: The server should return a 302 Redirect status code to the Google OAuth page.

Send a GET request to the /api/user/google endpoint via Postman.

Inspect the Status Code and Response Body in Postman.

column7: The server should return a 302 Redirect status code to the Google OAuth page.
column15: Send a GET request to /api/user/google/callback

column2: Verify that user can move task to another column successfully
column7:
1- Expected Result:

  • Status code should be 200/201
  • Column ID should be returned

2- Expected Result:

  • Status code should be 200/201
  • Task should be created successfully
  • Task ID should be returned

3- Expected Result:

  • Status code should be 200/201
  • Second column should be created successfully
  • Column ID should be returned

4- Expected Result:

  • Status code should be 200/201
  • Task should move successfully to selected column.
  • Task order should update correctly.
column15:
1- Create First Column

Request: POST /columns

Body Example:

{
  "title": "To Do"
}

2- Create Task in First Column

Request:

POST /tasks

Body Example:

{
  "title": "Test Task",
  "columnId": 1
}

3- Create Another Column

Request:

POST /columns

Body Example:

{
  "title": "Done"
}

4 - Move Task

Request:

PATCH /columns/tasks/{{taskID}}

Request Body:

{
  "column": "{{ColumnID}}",
  "newOrder": 3
}
